message: 🌱 Add radius: Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed by custom mission (#689)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "radius-6532-hard-to-use-self-signed-tls-certificates-signed-by-custom-ca",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "radius: Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed by custom CA",
+    "description": "Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed by custom CA. This issue affects 2+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify radius troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your radius deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n radius -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius -n radius --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for error: `code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: No such container: 05ec7676077a547ee6f6f6ae216128fb9b4ab37673c8baf9233`"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review radius configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant radius configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n radius -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius\nkubectl get configmap -n radius -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=radius\n```\n## Bug information\n\nI am happy to recreate the \"bug\" as a \"feature request\" given it isn't stated as supported anywhere on the docs. \n\nAs an employee at an enterprise company, we have a proxy running on our machines to support internal traffic."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed by custom",
+        "description": "Many enterprise customers leverage intermediate root certificate authorities (CAs) to enhance security and control over outgoing traffic originating from their employees' machines, particularly when using a firewall solution. However, when Radius users install Radius on local Kubernetes clusters in\n```yaml\n❯ rad init\n\"Selected No to create an application\"\n\nInitializing Radius. This may take a minute or two...\n\n✅ Install Radius v0.26.1\n   - Kubernetes cluster: rancher-desktop\n   - Kubernetes namespace: radius-system\n✅ Create new environment default\n   - Kubernetes namespace: default\n   - Recipe pack: local-dev\n✅ Update local configuration\n\nInitialization complete! Have a RAD time 😎\n\n❯ rad recipe list\nNAME      TYPE      TEMPLATE KIND  TEMPLATE VERSION  TEMPLATE\n\n❯ k get recipes.radapp.io -A\nNo resources found\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Hard to use self-signed TLS certificates / signed is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=radius -n radius --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n radius --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that `code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: No such container: 05ec7676077a547ee6f6f6ae216128fb9b4ab37673c8baf9233` no longer appears in logs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Many enterprise customers leverage intermediate root certificate authorities (CAs) to enhance security and control over outgoing traffic originating from their employees' machines, particularly when using a firewall solution. However, when Radius users install Radius on local Kubernetes clusters in their local machines under the corp network, the Radius executables utili",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "❯ rad init\n\"Selected No to create an application\"\n\nInitializing Radius. This may take a minute or two...\n\n✅ Install Radius v0.26.1\n   - Kubernetes cluster: rancher-desktop\n   - Kubernetes namespace: radius-system\n✅ Create new environment default\n   - Kubernetes namespace: default\n   - Recipe pack: local-dev\n✅ Update local configuration\n\nInitialization complete! Have a RAD time 😎\n\n❯ rad recipe list\nNAME      TYPE      TEMPLATE KIND  TEMPLATE VERSION  TEMPLATE\n\n❯ k get recipes.radapp.io -A\nNo resources found",
+        "❯ rad recipe register default --template-path ghcr.io/radius-project/recipes/local-dev/rediscaches:0.26.6 --resource-type Applications.Datastores/redisCaches --template-kind bicep\nSuccessfully linked recipe \"default\" to environment \"default\"",
+        "apiVersion: v1\ndata:\n  HTTP_PROXY: http://corp-proxy.com\n  HTTPS_PROXY: http://corp-proxy.com\n  NO_PROXY: .radius-system,controller.radius-system,ucp.radius-system,bicep-de.radius-system,applications-rp.radius-system,localhost,127.0.0.1,10.0.0.0/8,kubernetes\n  http_proxy: http://corp-proxy.com\n  https_proxy: http://corp-proxy.com\n  no_proxy: .radius-system,controller.radius-system,ucp.radius-system,bicep-de.radius-system,applications-rp.radius-system,localhost,127.0.0.1,10.0.0.0/8,kubernetes\nkind: ConfigMap\nmetadata:\n  name: proxy-env"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "radius",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "radius"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Deployment",
+      "Configmap",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/radius-project/radius/issues/6532",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/radius-project/radius",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/radius-project/radius/pull/6731"
+    },
+    "reactions": 2,
+    "comments": 12,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with radius installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-11T06:39:10.218Z",
